# add-gitlab-ci-cd-variables
Description: Add CI/CD variables to a GitLab repository for use in pipeline configurations.
Source: project
1. Navigate to your project in GitLab.
2. From the left sidebar, go to "Settings" and then select "CI/CD".
3. Expand the "Variables" section.
4. Click "Add variable".
5. Enter the `Key` (variable name) and `Value` for the variable.
6. Optionally, set the `Type` (e.g., Variable, File), `Environment scope`, and flags like `Protect variable` or `Mask variable`.
7. Click "Add variable" to save.
8. Repeat for all necessary variables.
